sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table(id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), created TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table(id, name, created) VALUES (1, 'Test', timestamp '2020-01-01 12:00:00');
INSERT INTO test_table(id, name, created) VALUES (2, 'Example', timestamp '2020-02-01 15:30:00');
INSERT INTO test_table(id, name, created) VALUES (3, '', timestamp '2020-03-01 18:45:00');
SELECT id, levenshtein(name, 'TestSample') AS distance, created FROM test_table WHERE levenshtein(name, 'TestSample') > 3;
SELECT id, created, levenshtein(name, 'EdgeCase') AS distance FROM test_table WHERE created > timestamp '2020-01-01 00:00:00' AND distance IS NOT NULL;
SELECT name, COUNT(*) FROM test_table GROUP BY name HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
CREATE TABLE another_table(id INT PRIMARY KEY, ref_id INT, FOREIGN KEY(ref_id) REFERENCES test_table(id));
SELECT t.id, t.name, a.ref_id, levenshtein(t.name, COALESCE(a.ref_id::VARCHAR, 'Unknown')) AS distance FROM test_table t LEFT JOIN another_table a ON t.id = a.ref_id WHERE distance < 5;
SELECT name, timestamp '2020-04-01 12:00:00' - created AS age_difference FROM test_table WHERE created IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, ROUND(levenshtein(name, 'Edge')) AS rounded_distance FROM test_table ORDER BY rounded_distance DESC;
SELECT id, LEAD(name) OVER (ORDER BY created) AS next_name FROM test_table;
SELECT id, created, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM another_table WHERE ref_id = test_table.id) AS ref_count FROM test_table;
SELECT levenshtein('ExtremeExamples', '') AS empty_distance, timestamp '2023-10-01 00:00:00' AS current_time;
